pi_items.source: Task specification in task.json and README.md
Task specification in task.json and README.md

Calculated: V_phase = V_line / √3 = 415 / 1.732050808 = 239.6003617 ≈ 239.6. Formula verified from authoritative electrical engineering sources (allaboutcircuits.com, vedantu.com, electricalacademia.com, electrical4u.com)

Calculated: I_phase = I_line / √3 = 63 / 1.732050808 = 36.373066959 ≈ 36.37. Formula verified from authoritative electrical engineering sources (electricaltechnology.org, allaboutcircuits.com, libretexts.org, eeeguide.com)
Tolerance value specified in README.md (Wye phase voltage ≈239.6 V ±3%, Delta phase current ≈36.37 A ±3%) and in verifier.py (TOLERANCE_PCT = 3.0)

privileged_info_summary: The task is a synthetic electrical engineering problem in which an agent must calculate 3-phase line-to-phase conversions for a 415 V, 63 A 3-phase supply. The metadata values are mathematically correct: the wye phase voltage is 239.6 V (calculated as 415 V / √3), and the delta phase current is 36.37 A (calculated as 63 A / √3). These formulas are standard in 3-phase electrical engineering and have been verified against authoritative electrical engineering sources. The tolerance for verification is 3%.
